content:
How to Apply to Nelson Mandela University (NMU)

Thinking about studying at Nelson Mandela University (NMU)? Based in Gqeberha (Port Elizabeth), Eastern Cape, NMU / Mandela University is a Comprehensive University established in 2005. This guide covers everything you need to know to apply successfully — from APS requirements to application deadlines, bursaries, and step-by-step instructions.

At a Glance: NMU / Mandela University Fast Facts

DetailInformation
Full NameNelson Mandela University (NMU)
TypeComprehensive University
LocationMultiple campuses in Gqeberha (Port Elizabeth); George Campus
Year Founded2005
Application Portalhttps://www.mandela.ac.za/Study-at-Mandela/How-to-apply
Application FeeR100
Applications OpenApril 1
Applications CloseSeptember 30
Minimum APS28 (varies by programme)
Contact Phone041 504 1111
Contact Emailadmissions@mandela.ac.za

Why Choose NMU / Mandela University?

Top 8 South African universities; named after South Africa's most celebrated leader.

  • Named after Nelson Mandela in 2005, merging the University of Port Elizabeth, PE Technikon, and Vista University
  • Home to Africa's only dedicated Faculty of Ocean Sciences
  • The George Campus serves the Garden Route region of the Western Cape
  • NMU's architecture programme is one of the top 3 in South Africa

Faculties and Programmes at NMU / Mandela University

NMU / Mandela University offers qualifications across 8 faculties:

  • Arts
  • Business & Economic Sciences
  • Education
  • Engineering, the Built Environment & Technology
  • Health Sciences
  • Law
  • Ocean Sciences
  • Science

Popular programmes include: BCom, BEng, LLB, BSc Ocean Sciences, Architecture.

APS Requirements for NMU / Mandela University

The minimum Admission Point Score (APS) required at NMU / Mandela University is 28 points. However, individual programmes may require significantly higher scores:

APS range: 28–38 depending on faculty

NMU requires a minimum APS of 28 for most programmes. Ocean Sciences and Engineering have stricter science subject requirements. The university offers access programmes for underprepared students.

How to Calculate Your APS

Your APS is calculated from your Grade 12 (NSC) subject results. Each subject receives a score from 1 to 7 based on your percentage:

Percentage (%)Rating CodeAPS Points
80–100%7 (Outstanding)7
70–79%6 (Meritorious)6
60–69%5 (Substantial)5
50–59%4 (Adequate)4
40–49%3 (Moderate)3
30–39%2 (Elementary)2
0–29%1 (Not Achieved)1

Your APS is the sum of your six best subjects (excluding Life Orientation, which is counted at half-value at most universities, or excluded entirely).

Use our free APS Calculator to calculate your score instantly.

Step-by-Step: How to Apply to NMU / Mandela University

Step 1: Check Programme Requirements

Before applying, confirm the specific APS requirements and subject prerequisites for your chosen programme at https://www.mandela.ac.za. Some programmes require specific subjects (e.g., Mathematics for Engineering, Life Sciences for Medicine).

Step 2: Gather Your Documents

You will typically need:

  • Certified copy of your ID (South African ID or passport for international students)
  • Certified copy of your Grade 11 final results (if applying before matric)
  • Certified copy of your NSC / Grade 12 results (final matric results when available)
  • Proof of residence (e.g., utility bill)
  • Completed application form (online or paper)

Step 3: Apply Online

Visit the NMU / Mandela University online application portal at https://www.mandela.ac.za/Study-at-Mandela/How-to-apply. Create an account, complete the application form, upload your documents, and pay the application fee of R100.

Step 4: Submit Before the Deadline

Applications for 2027 admission typically open in April 1 and close on September 30. Apply as early as possible — popular programmes fill up quickly, especially Medicine, Law, and Engineering.

Step 5: Accept Your Offer

Once you receive a conditional or unconditional offer from NMU / Mandela University, accept it within the specified timeframe (usually 2–4 weeks). You may need to pay a registration deposit to secure your place.

Step 6: Register for Your Programme

After accepting your offer, complete the formal registration process in January (or mid-year for Semester 2 programmes). Bring original and certified copies of all documents.

Application Deadlines for NMU / Mandela University

Missing the application deadline is one of the most common reasons students don't get into their chosen institution. Here are the key dates:

  • Applications Open: April 1
  • Application Deadline: September 30
  • Results Notification: Typically November–December for the following year
  • Registration: January (Semester 1) or mid-year

Pro tip: Don't wait until the last day. Apply when applications open — some programmes are filled on a first-come, first-served basis.

Bursaries and Financial Aid at NMU / Mandela University

Studying at NMU / Mandela University can be financed through several bursaries and financial aid options:

  • NSFAS
  • NMU Bursary Office
  • SAMSA (South African Maritime Safety Authority) for Ocean Sciences
  • Eastern Cape Development Corporation Bursaries

NSFAS (National Student Financial Aid Scheme): If your combined household income is below R350,000 per year, you may qualify for NSFAS funding. Apply at www.nsfas.org.za from October each year for the following academic year.

Preparing for NMU / Mandela University While Still in School

The best time to prepare for university is while you're still in Grade 10, 11, or 12. Here's what you can do:

  • Focus on your key subjects: Make sure you're strong in the subjects required for your chosen programme
  • Use AI tutoring: StudyBuddy's AI tutor helps you master difficult CAPS topics before matric
  • Research your options early: Visit open days at NMU / Mandela University to understand campus life
  • Apply for bursaries early: Some bursaries (like Allan Gray, Anglo American) are available while you're still in Grade 11
  • Track your APS: Use our APS calculator to know where you stand

What Happens After You Apply?

After submitting your application to NMU / Mandela University, the process typically follows these stages:

  1. Application Confirmation: You'll receive an email or SMS confirming receipt of your application
  2. Conditional Offer: If your Grade 11 results meet the requirements, you may receive a conditional offer
  3. Final Results Submission: Submit your final NSC results in January
  4. Unconditional Offer: If your matric results meet or exceed the requirements, your offer becomes unconditional
  5. Registration: Register for your programme and pay your fees or confirm NSFAS/bursary funding

Comparing NMU / Mandela University With Other Universities

Not sure if NMU / Mandela University is the right fit? Here are some things to consider:

FactorNMU / Mandela UniversityTypical Alternative
TypeComprehensive UniversityTraditional University / UoT
LocationGqeberha (Port Elizabeth)Varies
Minimum APS2820–37 (varies widely)
Application FeeR100R100–R400
NSFAS EligibleYesYes (public) / No (private)

Frequently Asked Questions About Applying to NMU / Mandela University

Still have questions? Our FAQ section below answers the most common questions about applying to NMU / Mandela University.

Need extra help with your Grade 12 subjects to meet NMU / Mandela University's entry requirements? StudyBuddy's AI tutor is FREE to start — get personalised help in Mathematics, Sciences, English, and all CAPS subjects. Most students improve their marks by 20–30% within a few weeks.

quickAnswer: To apply to Nelson Mandela University (NMU), visit https://www.mandela.ac.za/Study-at-Mandela/How-to-apply, pay the R100 application fee, and submit your documents before September 30. Minimum APS is 28 for most programmes.

faqs.question: What is the minimum APS to apply to NMU / Mandela University?
faqs.answer: The minimum APS at NMU / Mandela University is 28 points for most programmes. However, competitive programmes like Medicine, Law, or Engineering may require significantly higher scores (28–38 depending on faculty). Always check the specific requirements for your chosen programme on the NMU / Mandela University website.

faqs.question: When do applications open for NMU / Mandela University?
faqs.answer: Applications for NMU / Mandela University typically open in April 1 and close on September 30. We strongly recommend applying as early as possible, as popular programmes fill up quickly. Visit https://www.mandela.ac.za/Study-at-Mandela/How-to-apply to apply online.

faqs.question: How much is the application fee for NMU / Mandela University?
faqs.answer: The application fee at NMU / Mandela University is R100. Some students may qualify for a fee waiver — check the NMU / Mandela University website for details. The application fee is usually non-refundable even if your application is unsuccessful.

faqs.question: Does NMU / Mandela University accept NSFAS?
faqs.answer: Yes, NMU / Mandela University is an accredited NSFAS institution. If your household income is below R350,000 per year, you may qualify for NSFAS funding which can cover tuition, accommodation, meals, transport, and a living allowance. Apply at www.nsfas.org.za from October each year.

faqs.question: What documents do I need to apply to NMU / Mandela University?
faqs.answer: For your application to NMU / Mandela University, you will need: a certified copy of your ID, certified copies of your Grade 11 and/or Grade 12 results, proof of residence, and any programme-specific requirements (portfolio for arts, HPAT scores for health sciences, etc.). All documents should be certified within 3 months of the application date.
